name: Lasagna of Greens and Lemon
recipeIngredient: 1 bunch Swiss chard, rinsed, trimmed and chopped
1 bunch Mustard greens, rinsed, thick stems trimmed
1 bunch Kale, rinsed, trimmed and chopped
1/2 pound Brown mushrooms, rinsed, trimmed and quartered
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large sauté pan or wok, stir-fry Swiss chard, mustard greens, and kale with water clinging to leaves after rinsing. Cook the greens for 3-5 minutes until water is mostly absorbed.
2. Add minced garlic and cook until all greens are tender, about 10 minutes, stirring frequently to prevent burning. Add half of the lemon juice and stir until all moisture is reabsorbed. Season with salt and pepper.
3. Heat a non-stick sauté pan over high heat. When very hot, add mushrooms and cook, tossing and stirring constantly, until browned. Allow the mixture to cool completely; it should be dry.
4. In a large mixing bowl, combine ricotta, goat cheese, and lemon zest. Add the remaining half of lemon juice and stir to combine.
5. Once greens are completely cool, add them to the cheese mixture and stir well. Season with salt.
6. Assemble the lasagna: Spread 3/4 cup tomato puree on the bottom and sides of a glass baking pan. Cook pasta in boiling water until al dente, then drain. Lay one sheet of pasta at the bottom of the pan, covering it completely.
7. Spread half of the cheese mixture over the pasta sheet, sprinkle with one-third of mozzarella.
8. Repeat layering twice more, finishing with a third sheet of pasta. Spread remaining tomato puree over top and sprinkle with the rest of the mozzarella.
9. Bake at 300°F for 30-45 minutes until heated through.
Each serving contains about: 364 calories; 294 milligrams sodium; 110 milligrams cholesterol; 15 grams fat; 41 grams carbohydrates; 21 grams protein; 2.18 grams fiber.
